section_miscategorizations.text: The patient reports right upper quadrant tenderness.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is a patient-reported symptom rather than an exam finding; phrased as a subjective report, not an observed physical exam result.
Nausea here is clearly described as a patient-reported symptom, which belongs in the HPI rather than objective findings.
Fatigue is documented as a subjective complaint, not an observed exam or test result, so it fits HPI.
Chills are presented as a subjective symptom; they should be part of the HPI rather than the objective findings section.
This is more a statement of recent symptom history than a synthesized diagnostic impression; it would be clearer in HPI, though it does not strongly mislead in Assessment.

section_miscategorizations.text: A refill of lisinopril is requested.
section_miscategorizations.reason: As written, this reflects a patient request rather than a documented clinician plan or order; it fits better as part of the history/medication context unless explicitly accepted as a plan item.
